title: After betting the firm on Anthropic, Menlo Ventures raises victorious $3B fund
slides: Menlo bet everything on Anthropic. Did it pay off?
It raised a $3B fund after that single bet.
Most firms diversify. Menlo did the opposite.
If you're a founder, their conviction is now capital.
Ask: would you bet your firm on one company?
thread_text:
1. One bet, one firm

Menlo put its entire $3B fund into a single company: Anthropic. No backup, no hedge.

2. The anti-diversification play

Most VCs spread risk across 20+ startups. Menlo did the opposite and doubled down.

3. Conviction as capital

Founders now see Menlo’s move as a signal: extreme belief can unlock massive funding.

4. The bet behind the bet

Anthropic wasn’t a safe choice. Menlo chose a startup over safer AI giants.

5. Would you?

Ask yourself: could you bet your entire firm on one company? If not, what’s missing in your conviction?
video_script: They raised a three billion fund.
Conviction is now their only currency.
Would you bet your firm on one?

script: They raised a three billion fund.
Conviction is now their only currency.
Would you bet your firm on one?

instagram.caption:
One VC firm poured its entire $3 billion into a single AI startup—Anthropic—and just raised another $3 billion fund off the back of that bet. 🎲

No hedge. No backup. Most VCs spread risk across 20 companies; Menlo did the opposite and doubled down on one. That conviction turned into capital, signaling to founders that extreme belief can unlock massive backing.

For anyone building content or tools on AI models: this means Anthropic gets a huge cash injection to shape product features and pricing—your stack could depend on their roadmap.

Could you bet your entire operation on a single provider? What would it take for you to go all-in like that? 🔍
